string.text:
A Soviet 7.62x39mm semi-automatic carbine produced in the early 1950s. Cosmetically worn, but mechanically intact.
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255]Origin: %c[ui_gray_1]Soviet Union
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255]Manufacturer: %c[ui_gray_1]Tula Arsenal
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255]Production Year: %c[ui_gray_1]1951
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS:
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] None

A modern civilian variant of the SKS carbine, manufactured by Molot Arms for the Russian sporting and hunting market. The OP-SKS features a reinforced wood stock, improved finish, and can be fitted with a side-rail mount and a muzzle thread adapter to accept optics and modern suppressors.
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255]Origin: %c[ui_gray_1]Russia
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255]Manufacturer: %c[ui_gray_1]Molot Arms
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255]Production Year: %c[ui_gray_1]2000s–present
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS:
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] PSO-1, Kobra, 1P29 (after installing a Side-Rail Mount)
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] 7.62x39 compatible suppressor (after installing a Muzzle Thread Adapter)
A 7.62?39mm suppressor designed for SKS carbines.
A dovetail bracket for the SKS receiver, enabling installation of optics and reflex sights.
A threaded collar for the SKS muzzle, allowing attachment of modern 7.62?39 suppressors and muzzle devices.
OP-SKS Magazine with a capacity of 20 rounds.
